hero.title: Product design that ships and scales
hero.lede: Research, flows, and interfaces for software teams under pressure to grow. We design what the team can actually build.

problem.title: Design that looks good in Figma and falls apart in code
problem.p1: Mockups assume a perfect data state, ignore loading and empty cases, and ship to engineers who fill the gaps with guesses. Two sprints later the product looks nothing like the file.
problem.p2: Design systems get started, never finished. Components fork, color and spacing drift, and new hires re-learn the rules every quarter.
problem.p3: The cost is product velocity: every screen becomes a debate, and the team ships slower the bigger it gets.

solution.title: Design that holds up at the engineering handoff
solution.p1: We start with the user research and the flows, not the screens. Information architecture comes before pixels, and edge cases show up in the prototype, not in production.
solution.p2: We design components that map to real code, document the rules, and pair with engineering through implementation. The handoff is a conversation, not a throw-over.

bullets.b1: User research that drives decisions, not slide decks
bullets.b2: IA and flows that survive contact with engineering
bullets.b3: Design system founding work — tokens, components, documentation
bullets.b4: Interaction and motion design for states the team usually forgets
bullets.b5: Figma libraries that map cleanly to your component framework

solution.visualAlt: Layered Figma frames showing flows, components, and a design system in use

uc1.title: Product redesign under traction
uc1.description: Refactor the interface without rebuilding the product. Keep what works, fix what blocks growth.

uc2.title: Design system founding work

uc3.title: Dashboard or admin tool

howItWorks.title: From research to a system the team can run with
howItWorks.footnote: Typical engagement: 8–14 weeks, depending on system scope.

s1.label: Research and audit
s1.detail: We talk to users, audit the existing product, and map the problems worth solving first.

s2.label: IA and flows
s2.detail: We design the structure and journeys, validate them in low-fi, and align with engineering before pixels.

s3.label: Interface and system
s3.detail: We design the interface, build the component library, and document the rules in Figma and prose.

s4.label: Handoff and pairing
s4.detail: We pair with engineering through build, refine in code, and leave a system the team can extend.

i1.title: Faster product velocity
i1.description: Engineers stop guessing. New screens are assembled from components in hours, not days.

i2.title: Consistency that lasts
i2.description: A documented system means the tenth designer makes the same decisions as the first.

i3.title: Real user evidence
i3.description: Decisions backed by research and prototype testing, not opinion in review meetings.

i4.title: Design that ships intact
i4.description: What’s in Figma is what reaches production — including loading, empty, and error states.

q1.q: Do you do user research, or just design?
q1.a: Both — and they connect. Research shapes the design brief, and prototypes feed back into research. The depth scales with budget; we’ll recommend what’s worth funding.

q2.q: Can you work alongside our in-house designers?
q2.a: Yes. We often join existing teams as a strike force on a specific surface, system, or redesign — not to replace anyone, but to ship the work that’s been queued.

q3.q: Do you build the design system, or just design components?
q3.a: We design the system, document the rules, and pair with engineering on the implementation. The library lives in your tools and your codebase, not ours.

q4.a: Engineering is in the loop from week one — not at handoff. We pair through build, review the code against the design, and fix mismatches together.

q5.q: Can you work in our existing Figma setup?
q5.a: Yes. We work in your file structure, your naming, and your branching model. If those don’t exist yet, we propose a setup that scales without rebuilding later.

cta.title: Let’s scope the work
cta.lede: Tell us what the product is and where it’s stuck. We’ll come back with a plan within two business days.

hero.title: A clear plan and the metrics to prove it
hero.lede: We help founders and brands set the next quarter’s priority, ship against it, and measure what changed. No deck-ware.

problem.title: Strategy as slides, not as a plan you can run
problem.p1: Most strategy work ends in a 60-page deck nobody reads twice. The real questions — what to ship next quarter, which channel to fund, what proves the bet worked — go unanswered.
problem.p2: Founders pick a positioning by gut. Growth teams run channels they inherited. Brand teams write content nobody asked for. Effort goes in, signal does not come out.
problem.p3: The cost is wasted quarters: campaigns that don’t compound, hires aimed at the wrong gap, and a roadmap that drifts toward whoever spoke last.

solution.title: Strategy as the next quarter’s priority and how it’s measured
solution.p1: We start where you are: the product, the audience, the numbers that exist today. We name the one or two bets worth making this quarter, and the metrics that will show whether they worked.
solution.p2: Then we shape the work around those bets — positioning, content, channel mix, roadmap — and stay in the room while the team executes. Strategy that ends at the deck is half the job.

bullets.b1: Positioning and messaging grounded in customer language, not internal jargon
bullets.b2: Channel and content mix tied to a measurement framework you can read
bullets.b3: Roadmap shaping — what to ship, what to cut, what to defer
bullets.b4: OKRs or KPIs that mean something, with weekly read-outs the team trusts
bullets.b5: Go-to-market plans for launches, repositions, and category shifts

solution.visualAlt: Strategy framework showing positioning, channels, and measurement connected to a quarterly roadmap

uc1.title: Pre-launch positioning
uc1.description: Founders shaping the narrative, channels, and first-quarter targets before they go to market.

uc2.title: Brand reposition
uc2.description: Established brands moving up-market, into a new category, or away from a positioning that’s stopped paying.

uc3.title: Growth team without a strategist
uc3.description: Strong execution, missing direction. We set the bets and the measurement, then hand the wheel back.

uc4.title: Content and channel rebuild
uc4.description: Replace the calendar nobody reads with a plan tied to pipeline, distribution, and a measurable thesis.

howItWorks.title: From audit to a plan the team can ship
howItWorks.footnote: Typical engagement: 4–8 weeks for the plan, optional ongoing partnership for execution.

s1.label: Audit and interviews
s1.detail: We review the product, the numbers, and the market, and talk to customers, the team, and the leaders.

s2.label: Positioning and bets
s2.detail: We name the positioning, the audience, and the one or two bets worth funding this quarter.

s3.label: Plan and measurement
s3.detail: We shape channels, content, and roadmap around the bets, with metrics the team can read weekly.

s4.label: Run and review
s4.detail: We stay in the room — weekly check-ins, mid-quarter review, course-correction before the quarter ends.

i1.title: A plan, not a deck
i1.description: The output is a working document the team uses every week, not a PDF that ages in a shared drive.

i2.title: Metrics that mean something
i2.description: Each bet has a number attached. You’ll know within a quarter whether it worked — and what to do next.

i3.title: Faster decisions
i3.description: Positioning, audience, and priorities are written down. New hires, agencies, and partners get the brief in one read.

i4.title: Compounding, not churn
i4.description: Quarter on quarter, the work builds on itself instead of restarting from scratch.

q1.a: Both, and we’re clear about the line. We shape the plan, then either hand it to your team or stay on as a partner through the quarter. We won’t do strategy that’s never executed.

q2.q: How is this different from a brand or growth agency?
q2.a: Brand and growth agencies execute well in their lane. We sit upstream of both — naming what should be done, in what order, with what budget. Then we pick the right partners to execute, in-house or external.

q3.q: Do you work with pre-revenue founders?
q3.a: Yes, often. The work is leaner — positioning, audience, first channel, first metric — and the goal is to ship the first version with the right shape, not the perfect one.

q4.q: What does measurement look like in practice?
q4.a: A small dashboard the team reads weekly, two or three numbers per bet, and a written read-out every other week. Not 40 KPIs, not a BI build.

q5.q: Can you work with our agencies and freelancers?
q5.a: Yes. Strategy is most useful when the existing partners get a clearer brief. We write the brief, sit in the kickoff, and review the output against the plan.

cta.title: Let’s name the next bet
cta.lede: Tell us where the business is and what’s stuck. We’ll come back with a scoping plan within two business days.

hero.title: Ship the product the design promised
hero.lede: We make slow products fast, brittle codebases stable, and audits pass on the first try. The engineering side that delivers what design and strategy planned.

problem.title: Where products lose users without anyone noticing
problem.p1: The site looks finished and the analytics dashboard looks fine. Meanwhile real users on real networks bounce — the page took four seconds, the form didn’t submit on a flaky connection, the screen reader hit a dead end.
problem.p2: Codebases inherited from earlier teams hide their cost: every release breaks something, the test suite is decorative, and one engineer holds the deploy in their head.
problem.p3: By the time it shows up in revenue, the gap between what was shipped and what was promised is months of rework.

solution.title: Measure first, fix what matters, prove it changed
solution.p1: We start with measurement on real devices and real networks — not synthetic lab runs. Core Web Vitals, accessibility audits, error budgets, and the parts of the codebase that quietly cost the team time.
solution.p2: Then we fix in priority order: the issues that move the metric, not the long tail. Each change is shipped behind monitoring, with the before-and-after recorded so the team can see what worked.

bullets.b1: Core Web Vitals tuned on real-user data, not just Lighthouse
bullets.b2: Accessibility to WCAG AA, validated with assistive tech, not just a checker
bullets.b3: Observability — logs, traces, error budgets — wired into the team’s daily work
bullets.b4: CI/CD pipelines that catch regressions before they ship, not after
bullets.b5: Compliance-grade ops for regulated industries — audit trails, runbooks, on-call

solution.visualAlt: Performance dashboard showing Core Web Vitals trending downward as real users see faster pages

uc1.title: Slow product or failing audits
uc1.description: A diagnosis, a prioritized fix list, and the engineering work to land it — measured before and after.

uc2.title: Inherited brittle codebase
uc2.description: Stabilize a codebase mid-flight: tests, CI, observability, and the refactors that pay back inside a quarter.

uc3.title: Regulated-industry delivery
uc3.description: Banking, health, govtech: compliance-grade ops, audit trails, and release processes regulators accept.

uc4.title: Accessibility remediation
uc4.description: Move from failing audits to WCAG AA conformance, validated with screen readers and keyboard testing — not just automated tools.

howItWorks.title: From audit to a measurable improvement
howItWorks.footnote: Typical engagement: 4–10 weeks, depending on the codebase size and the metric gap.

s1.label: Audit and baseline
s1.detail: We measure performance, accessibility, and code health on real devices, then write a baseline you can compare against.

s2.label: Prioritize
s2.detail: We rank the fixes by impact and effort, name the ones that move the metric, and defer the ones that don’t.

s3.label: Ship and monitor
s3.detail: We land the changes behind feature flags, watch real-user metrics, and roll back if anything regresses.

s4.label: Hand over
s4.detail: We document the system, set up the dashboards, and leave the team able to keep the metric where we put it.

i1.title: Measurable speed gains
i1.description: Core Web Vitals reported before and after, on real users — not synthetic scores.

i2.title: Audits that pass
i2.description: WCAG AA conformance and clean Lighthouse on the first regulator or procurement review.

i3.title: Code that doesn’t fight back
i3.description: CI/CD, tests, and observability that turn deploys from events into routine.

i4.title: A team that owns it
i4.description: Dashboards, runbooks, and documentation so the work doesn’t unwind the week we leave.

q1.q: How do you measure performance — Lighthouse?
q1.a: Lighthouse is a starting point. We tune against real-user metrics — Core Web Vitals from your actual traffic — because lab scores often miss what real users feel. Both numbers go in the report.

q2.q: Do you rewrite the codebase or work with what we have?
q2.a: We work with what you have. Rewrites are slow, expensive, and rarely fix the underlying issue. We refactor in place, with tests, until the metric moves.

q3.a: WCAG AA conformance, validated with screen readers (NVDA, VoiceOver), keyboard testing, and color-contrast checks. Automated tools catch maybe 30%; we cover the rest.

q4.q: Do you handle on-call and incident response?
q4.a: We set up the systems — dashboards, alerts, runbooks — and we can stay on retainer for incident support. Long-term on-call usually belongs with your team; we make that handover clean.

q5.q: Can you work with our existing CI/CD and observability stack?
q5.a: Yes. We work with whatever you run — GitHub Actions, GitLab, Jenkins; Datadog, Grafana, Sentry. The goal is to make your stack work better, not to swap it.

cta.title: Let’s measure what’s slowing you down
cta.lede: Tell us what the product is and where it hurts. We’ll come back with a baseline plan within two business days.

about.title: About Lunatech
about.p1: Lunatech is a creative team focused on building and evolving digital products.
about.p2: We work at the intersection of design and technology to deliver functional, aesthetic, and lasting solutions for brands.
about.p3: Our goal isn’t just to exist online — it’s to make a difference.

approach.title: Our approach
approach.p1: We look at every project through a strategic lens.
approach.p2: We balance aesthetics and performance to ship products that create real user value.
approach.p3: For us, design isn’t only how it looks — it’s how it works.

process.title: Process
process.discoverDetail: Align on goals, constraints, and audiences before we shape the work.
process.designDetail: Structure, visuals, and interaction — crafted for clarity and conversion.
process.buildDetail: Engineering with polish: performance, accessibility, QA, and resilient delivery.
process.launchDetail: Ship, measure, and iterate with a clear handover and support plan.
process.subtitle: Each phase is planned and transparent.
